data.title: Implement sprint-review-scope-check.sh script
data.description:
## Description
Create sprint-review-scope-check.sh that compares reviewer-findings.json file references against the task file impact table to detect out-of-scope review feedback.

## TDD Requirement
RED tests from task 6af2-fd9b must be written first. This task makes those tests pass (GREEN).

## Implementation
Input: task-id and path to reviewer-findings.json
1. Read the task ticket to extract ## File Impact section (parse multiple heading formats per gap analysis from story 119b-f4ab)
2. Parse reviewer-findings.json to extract file paths from accepted findings
3. Compare: for each file in reviewer findings, check if it exists in the task file impact table
4. Collect files that are in findings but NOT in the file impact table

Output:
- IN_SCOPE (exit 0) if all finding files are in task scope
- OUT_OF_SCOPE: <comma-separated file list> (exit 0) if out-of-scope files found
- Exit 1 on missing arguments, exit 2 on usage error

Edge cases:
- No reviewer-findings.json → IN_SCOPE (graceful)
- Task has no file impact section → IN_SCOPE (cannot determine scope)
- Empty findings → IN_SCOPE

## Files to Create
- plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-review-scope-check.sh

## Files to Modify
- .test-index (remove RED markers)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script file exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-review-scope-check.sh
- [ ] Script handles missing arguments gracefully
  Verify: bash plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-review-scope-check.sh 2>/dev/null; test $? -ne 0
- [ ] All RED tests from 6af2-fd9b pass
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-sprint-review-scope-check.sh
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
